L1.area: Administration panel settings and team management functionality

L2.description: Two authorization defects allow any authenticated panel user to take over the role-based access control system. One component lacks page-level authorization entirely, allowing any authenticated user to invoke privileged actions such as creating new roles and deleting other users including administrators. Another component gates its write operations on a read-only permission, meaning any user holding a basic view capability can grant themselves or others arbitrary permissions, effectively escalating to full panel administrator from a read-only account.

L3.area: Team and role management settings in the admin panel
L3.description: Two authorization defects in the team settings allow any authenticated panel user to take over the role-based access control system. One team settings component lacks an authorization gate during component initialization, allowing any authenticated user to invoke privileged actions such as creating new roles and deleting administrators. The role-permission editing component gates its write operations (saving role data, generating permissions, creating new permissions) on a read-only permission instead of a management-level permission, meaning any user holding basic view access can grant themselves or others arbitrary permissions, effectively escalating to full panel administrator.

ground_truth.reason: Settings/Team/Index lacked any mount() authorization allowing any authenticated user to create roles and delete administrators, and Settings/Team/RolePermission gated write actions on the read-only view_users permission instead of a privileged permission, enabling privilege escalation to full administrator.
